summary: Plan is architecturally sound and integrates cleanly with the existing 4-system ModeManager pattern. Three conditions must be resolved before implementation: the CPU/GPU displacement duplication, missing debug overlay wiring, and InstancedMesh + custom ShaderMaterial vertex transform handling.

interface_changes.impact: Non-breaking additive field. Existing consumers unaffected since they don't reference maxInstances.

conditions.title: CPU/GPU displacement duplication must be resolved
conditions.detail: Step 3 (vertex shader) describes bass displacement via curl3 in GLSL. Step 5 (draw loop) describes the identical bass displacement via curl3 on CPU updating instance matrices. Both displace instance positions by curl3 scaled by bassEnergy — executing both would double-displace. Since the plan's core decision is 'CPU-side matrix updates via Float32Array' and the ticket specifies 'Instance matrices are updated per frame from a Float32Array', the vertex shader should NOT independently apply curl3 displacement to instance positions. The vertex shader should apply instanceMatrix faithfully, add only minor per-vertex effects (e.g. treble shimmer on individual vertices within a shape), and handle coloring/fog. All macro bass displacement belongs in the CPU-side matrix update loop.
conditions.recommendation: Remove curl3/bass displacement from the vertex shader (step 3). Keep snoise-based treble micro-perturbation in the shader for per-vertex shimmer within each instance shape. Move all macro displacement to CPU-side matrix updates (step 5).

conditions.title: InstancedMesh + custom ShaderMaterial requires manual instanceMatrix handling
conditions.detail: Three.js built-in materials automatically apply the instanceMatrix attribute in the vertex shader via shader chunks (#include <begin_vertex>, #include <project_vertex>). A custom ShaderMaterial does NOT inject these chunks. The vertex shader must manually: (1) declare 'attribute mat4 instanceMatrix;' if not using THREE.GLSL3, or rely on Three.js v0.183.2 auto-injection for InstancedMesh, (2) multiply the vertex position by instanceMatrix before modelViewMatrix. The plan's vertex shader spec in step 3 does not mention this transform chain. Incorrect handling will render all instances at the origin.
conditions.recommendation: The vertex shader must include: 'vec4 instancePos = instanceMatrix * vec4(position, 1.0); vec4 mvPosition = modelViewMatrix * instancePos;' Three.js r183 auto-declares instanceMatrix for InstancedMesh with ShaderMaterial, so no explicit attribute declaration is needed, but the multiplication must be explicit in the GLSL.

conditions.title: Debug overlay modes array not updated
conditions.detail: main.ts lines 135-140 define a modes array for the debug overlay that maps activeIndex to mode name and point count. The plan adds a 5th system to ModeManager but does not include updating this debug metadata array. If omitted, activeIndex=4 will return 'unknown' in the debug overlay.
conditions.recommendation: Add { name: 'microgeometry', maxPoints: quality.maxInstances } to the modes array in main.ts alongside the ModeManager entry.

strengths.title: Clean additive integration
strengths.detail: Adding a 5th GeometrySystem follows the exact same pattern as the existing 4 systems. ModeManager accepts an array of ModeEntry — no structural changes to the manager, transition logic, or health gate are required.

strengths.title: Correct reuse of existing infrastructure
strengths.detail: Plan reuses computeAdaptiveCount (exported from pointCloud.ts), volumetricPoints generators for initial placement, noise3d.glsl for shader functions, shaderRegistry COMMON_UNIFORMS pattern, and PRNG seeding — all consistent with established conventions.

strengths.title: Appropriate technology choice
strengths.detail: InstancedMesh is the correct Three.js primitive for many small identical shapes. Single draw call regardless of instance count. GPU cost scales with vertex count × instances rather than draw calls × instances.

strengths.title: Health gate compatibility
strengths.detail: The existing health gate traverses the scene for BufferGeometry objects and validates position attributes. InstancedMesh.geometry is a BufferGeometry with a position attribute, so it passes validation without modifications to the gate.

strengths.title: Quality tier scaling is well-calibrated
strengths.detail: Instance counts (200/600/1200) are appropriately lower than point counts (200/800/2000) since each instance has 12-36 vertices versus 1 vertex per point. GPU cost is roughly equivalent per tier.

risks.risk: CPU-side matrix loop performance at 1200 instances
risks.detail: 1200 × Matrix4.compose() per frame at 60fps = 72,000 compose calls/sec. Each compose involves quaternion → matrix conversion (~40 multiplications). Pre-allocated objects mitigate GC but not arithmetic cost. On low-end devices this could consume 2-4ms per frame.
risks.mitigation: Plan already includes optimization step (step 6) with pre-allocated objects and alternating-frame updates for low tier. Consider also simplifying displacement calculation on low tier (linear interpolation instead of curl noise).

risks.risk: Shader compile failure with InstancedMesh-specific GLSL
risks.detail: Custom ShaderMaterial on InstancedMesh is a less-tested path than Points + ShaderMaterial. The instanceMatrix attribute injection behavior varies across Three.js versions and may differ from the plan's assumptions.
risks.mitigation: Existing ShaderErrorCollector and health gate will catch compile failures. Test shader in isolation before wiring into ModeManager. Verify instanceMatrix availability with a minimal InstancedMesh + ShaderMaterial test.

risks.risk: Crossfade visual artifacts between instanced meshes and point-based systems
risks.detail: Existing systems use AdditiveBlending + depthWrite:false on Points. InstancedMesh with the same blending may produce different visual weight during crossfade due to denser screen coverage per instance. Opacity crossfade may feel uneven.
risks.mitigation: Test crossfade visually. May need to adjust opacity curve or blending mode for the micro-geometry system specifically.

modify.scope: Add maxInstances field to interface + 3 tier objects
Import + instantiate MicroGeometry, add to ModeManager array, update debug modes array

no_change_needed.reason: ModeManager is parameterized by ModeEntry[] — accepts any number of systems without code changes. Plan incorrectly lists this as needing modification.

plan_corrections.correction: ModeManager accepts ModeEntry[] via createModeManager() — no changes to modeManager.ts are needed. The new system is wired entirely in main.ts.

plan_corrections.issue: Vertex shader spec includes bass macro displacement via curl3, duplicating CPU-side displacement in step 5
plan_corrections.correction: See condition C1. Vertex shader should apply instanceMatrix transform and only do per-vertex micro effects (treble shimmer). All macro displacement belongs in CPU-side matrix updates.

plan_corrections.issue: No mention of instanceMatrix multiplication in vertex shader transform chain
plan_corrections.correction: See condition C2. Must explicitly multiply by instanceMatrix before modelViewMatrix in custom ShaderMaterial.

plan_corrections.issue: Does not mention updating debug overlay modes array in main.ts
plan_corrections.correction: See condition C3. Must add microgeometry entry to the modes array at main.ts:135-140.

US-041.detail: noise3d.glsl with curl3/fbm3/snoise confirmed present and used by all 4 existing systems

US-047.detail: computeAdaptiveCount exported from pointCloud.ts, already imported by crystalField.ts — reuse pattern established

US-048.detail: ShaderErrorCollector, healthGate, initAllForValidation all wired in renderLoop.ts and functioning for existing 4 systems
